focus: Produce the final dependency-ordered resource map and implementation handoff with explicit validity transitions, acceptance gates, and residual decisions.
noveltyJustification: 2 of 5 findings were fully new and 3 partially new (0.70), plus a 0.10 simplicity bonus for reducing the remaining work to one dependency DAG.
keyQuestions: Which exact resource and dependency map gives the implementation packet a no-further-research execution order?
answeredQuestions: Which exact resource and dependency map gives the implementation packet a no-further-research execution order?
ruledOut: Running the benchmark before loader, manifest, topology, and fallback gates pass
Treating router-derived candidates as author-approved gold
Promoting loader-ineligible files for the first slice

label: Eight-gate implementation DAG orders baseline, loader support, index repair, manifest, typed gold, topology, router safety, and benchmark comparison.
Static, loader, manifest, executable-oracle, observed-route, and score-comparable validity have distinct transition gates.
The proposed seed spans seven rows, seven modes, and 43 candidate typed pairs, subject to fixture-author approval.
Acceptance requires combined structural, oracle, routing, safety, and same-corpus scoring evidence.
Only loader-shape selection and fixture-author approval remain; loader-ineligible promotion is optional governance.
Canonical typed identity remains workflowMode plus child-local leafResourceId; fallback behavior is not weakened.

reason: Scoring before loader, manifest, topology, and fallback gates cannot distinguish contract defects from routing quality.
Router-derived leaves require explicit fixture-author approval before becoming oracle gold.

direction: Loader-ineligible corpus promotion on the critical path
reason: The seven-row slice closes mode coverage without promoting any of the 273 ineligible files.
